summary: Independent QA reviewed the actual branch/worktree diff for M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001. The M4 deterministic replay fixture covers objective creation, muster response, grain reservation, march execution, automatic engagement, siege choices through surrender, objective-complete withdrawal, war outcome, and postwar candidate creation. Node and worker-compatible paths use the same protocol script and produce matching final, engagement, siege, and withdrawal hashes. M4 save-format v1 persistence remains intentionally unsupported, with explicit save rejection and safe missing-M4 load rejection without runtime mutation.

acceptance.criterion: Actual diff only touches allowed paths and no forbidden paths.
acceptance.evidence: Checked committed diff from origin/main, working tree diff, and untracked files. All paths were under task allowed_paths; no forbidden_paths were touched.

acceptance.criterion: Deterministic replay covers the complete M4 loop.
acceptance.evidence: createM4DeterminismReplayScriptV1 contains 12 accepted commands: objective, muster commitment, muster response, grain reservation, march start, two day advances, field engagement, invest-blockade, assault, accept-surrender, and objective-complete withdrawal. Independent replay produced 1 campaign plan, 1 commitment, 2 grain reservations, 1 march, 1 field engagement, 1 siege, 1 withdrawal, 1 war outcome, and 1 postwar candidate.

acceptance.criterion: Node and browser Worker-compatible paths produce matching M4 hashes from the same seed and command sequence.
acceptance.evidence: pnpm sim:determinism reported Node M4 hash 232f62ab and Worker-compatible M4 hash 232f62ab; engagement hash 334e0df0, siege hash ac35e97e, withdrawal hash 232f62ab.

acceptance.criterion: Automatic engagement/siege replay has stable casualty/supply/result hashes.
acceptance.evidence: tests/save-load-contract.test.ts asserts the replayed field engagement casualties, supply loss, outcome reason codes, siege surrender state, withdrawal, and postwar candidate. The runner also compares engagement/siege/withdrawal hashes across Node and worker-compatible execution.

acceptance.criterion: M4 runtime save/load boundary is explicit and safe.
acceptance.evidence: requestSaveV1 rejects runtime.world.state.m4 with an explicit M4 save-format v1 unsupported error before creating a lossy save. loadSaveV1 rejects missing-M4 snapshots for M4 runtimes with semantic-invariant at state.m4 and returns the original runtime unchanged.

acceptance.criterion: No product gameplay, UI, telemetry, Electron/Node integration, networking, server, multiplayer, manual battle, 3D, or arbitrary-code mod scope expansion.
acceptance.evidence: Diff is limited to deterministic fixture/test/protocol runner paths and process state files. Risk scan found no changed-code hits for Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, nodeIntegration, telemetry, networking/server patterns, manual battle, 3D, eval/new Function, arbitrary-code, unsafe double assertion, or any; only existing observer and expect.any matches appeared.

acceptance.criterion: Required tests are real and discriminating.
acceptance.evidence: The added tests fail on hash mismatch, missing postwar candidate, wrong combat/siege/withdrawal details, non-rejection of M4 save, or runtime mutation on rejected load. Required commands were rerun successfully by QA.

tests_run.result: PASS; branch test/m4-determinism-replay with expected modified and untracked files only.

tests_run.command: git diff / git ls-files allowed-path check
tests_run.result: PASS; all committed, working tree, and untracked paths are within allowed_paths and outside forbidden_paths.
PASS; Node and worker-compatible M4 hash 232f62ab matched, with engagement 334e0df0, siege ac35e97e, withdrawal 232f62ab, and 1 postwar candidate.
PASS; architecture check, determinism runner, root vitest 33 files / 239 tests, and web package 1 file / 3 tests.
PASS; format check, lint, typecheck, and architecture boundaries.
PASS; task JSON valid in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001
tests_run.result: PASS; id/status/route_to checked as M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001 / REVIEW / qa_reviewer.

tests_run.command: risk scan for nondeterminism and forbidden scope keywords in changed code
tests_run.result: PASS; only existing observer strings and expect.any test matchers were reported.
PASS; verified command sequence, final result, per-stage hashes, and M4 collection counts.

decisions: ACCEPT: actual diff, replay coverage, Node/worker-compatible hash parity, save/load boundary behavior, and required tests satisfy M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001 acceptance criteria.

Accepted the explicit M4 save-format v1 rejection boundary because requestSaveV1 rejects M4 runtime state before lossy serialization and loadSaveV1 rejects missing-M4 snapshots without replacing the runtime.
risks: M4 runtime persistence is still intentionally unsupported in save-format v1; future save-format work must add schema-backed M4 DTOs and compatibility tests before enabling M4 save writes.
The browser-side parity path remains the existing worker-compatible adapter pattern, not a newly spawned browser Worker test; this matches the project canary path used by the current codebase.
requested_action: Proceed with lead integration gates for M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001.
